_about: Eval cases for Piece 2 — the LHS/RHS numeric sub-decomposer. Each case is (description, kind, expected_spec). Input is a free-text description from the Stage-1 classifier (e.g., '9.12% of the Salary Cap'). Output is a structured spec tree the engine conversion stage can use.
_decisions_referenced: Decision 3: definitional inlining already done by Stage-1; sub-decomposer trusts inlined form
Decision 4: aggregates route to derived_atom (Map-resolved), NOT engine SumNodes
Decision 5: max-of-formulas route to derived_atom (Map-resolved), NOT engine MaxNodes

_node_semantics_reference.TimesConstNode: child × constant — used for 'X percent of Y' patterns
_node_semantics_reference.PlusConstNode: child + constant — used for 'Y plus $C' patterns
_node_semantics_reference.MinusConstNode: child − constant — used for 'Y minus $C' patterns
_node_semantics_reference.ConstMinusNode: constant − child — used for 'cap minus Y' patterns

cases.context: Direct reference to a case-data numeric attribute.
Compound noun phrase referring to a single numeric attribute.
Bare integer constant. Should produce a value=4 spec.
Named CBA constant. The 2024-25 value is $140,588,000. The spec should record the label; downstream knows the value.
Bare dollar constant. Should produce value=250000.
The MLE limit pattern. Engine-expressible as TimesConst(0.0912, Constant(salary_cap)).
The 105%-of-prior pattern. Engine-expressible as TimesConst(1.05, NumericLeaf(prior_salary)).
The TPE allowance pattern. Engine-expressible as PlusConst(NumericLeaf(outgoing), 250000). The child is a numeric leaf; constant is 250000.

cases.description: the salary cap minus the team's current salary
aggregate first-year Salaries and Unlikely Bonuses of all Player Contracts signed under the Non-Taxpayer Mid-Level Salary Exception
cases.context: Per Decision 4, aggregates over multiple instances are not engine SumNodes. They are derived numeric atoms that Map computes. The spec should record the description and tag as aggregate_sum.

cases.description: the greater of 25% of the Salary Cap or 105% of the player's prior-year salary
cases.context: Per Decision 5, max-of-formulas are derived atoms (no engine MaxNode). The spec should record the description and tag as max_of.

cases.description: the player's Maximum Annual Salary under Article II, Section 7
cases.context: A reference to a named derived quantity. The full computation lives in Section 7. The spec should treat this as a derived atom — Map computes the value by reading Section 7's formula and the player's attributes.
Stage-1 may sometimes tag a bare named constant as 'arithmetic' instead of 'constant'. The sub-decomposer should be robust: recognize this is actually a constant and produce a constant spec.
cases._eval_note: Acceptable also: derived_atom (treating cap as a named quantity). Either is recoverable downstream.

cases.description: 9.12% of (the Salary Cap minus the prior-year contract salary)
cases.context: Nested arithmetic: TimesConst(0.0912, child=ConstMinus(constant=cap, child=NumericLeaf(prior))). Tests whether the sub-decomposer correctly recurses on the inner arithmetic.

scoring_levels: Level 1 (mandatory): spec_type matches (numeric_leaf / constant / unary_arithmetic / derived_atom).
Level 2 (preferred): for unary_arithmetic, operator matches; for constant, value or label is correct.
Level 3 (informational): atom_id_hint is reasonable; computation_kind is correct for derived_atom.
decisions_under_test: Will Opus correctly route aggregates and max-of-formulas to derived_atom?
Will Opus correctly identify the engine-expressible operator for each unary arithmetic case?
Will Opus recurse into nested arithmetic correctly?
Will Opus handle the adversarial mistagging case gracefully?
